claims.claim: Visitors who register an account in the system are referred to as 'members'
claims.evidence: MemberInfo class in CMSSolution/Membership/Members/MemberRoleInfo.cs; CMS.Member system table exists. Standard Xperience terminology.

claims.claim: Members can be assigned to 'member roles' that control which content they can view
claims.evidence: MemberRoleMemberInfo.cs is a binding between members and roles (IsBinding=true, DependsOn MemberInfo). ContentAccessSettings supports SecuredWithRoles tier per AREA-002.
claims.docFact: AREA-002: MemberRoleInfo and MemberRoleMemberInfo types support role creation and member-role assignment.

claims.claim: Member accounts and member roles are managed using the 'Members' application
claims.evidence: MembersApplication.cs: UIApplication attribute registers app with caption '{$base.members.application$}'. Resource string base.members.application = 'Members'. MemberList and MemberRoleList are child pages.
claims.docFact: AREA-012: Member role admin UI pages (listing/create/edit) already present.

claims.claim: The application's home page lists all member accounts in the system
claims.evidence: MemberList.cs: UIPage(typeof(MembersApplication), 'list', typeof(MemberList), ..., UIPageOrder.First). ObjectType = MemberInfo.OBJECT_TYPE. First child = default landing.

claims.claim: Member detail fields: 'Member name', 'Email', 'Enabled'
claims.evidence: Member edit.xml: MemberName caption '{$base.members.edit.name$}'='Member name'; MemberEmail caption '{$base.members.edit.email$}'='Email'; MemberEnabled caption '{$base.members.edit.enabled$}'='Enabled'.

claims.claim: Member name — the name used by the member to sign in. Modifying this value may change the sign-in credentials
claims.evidence: MemberInfo.MemberName is the identity field used by ASP.NET Identity stores (ApplicationUserStore). The 'may change' qualifier is correct — behavior depends on the site's identity configuration.

claims.claim: Email — the email used by the member to sign in. Modifying this value may change the sign-in credentials
claims.evidence: MemberInfo.MemberEmail is used as secondary identity. Same 'may change' qualifier applies.

claims.claim: Enabled — determines whether the member account is allowed to sign in
claims.evidence: MemberInfo.MemberEnabled field. MemberList.cs has EnableMember/DisableMember commands that toggle this field.

claims.claim: Users that create an account using an external provider (e.g., Google or Facebook) cannot be edited via the Members application
claims.evidence: MemberEdit.cs lines 60-76: when member.MemberIsExternal==true, MemberName and MemberEmail fields set to FormEditMode.Disabled. The core identity fields are locked. Note: MemberEnabled toggle and Membership roles remain editable for external accounts, but the primary account fields (name, email) referenced in the 'View member details' section above are indeed non-editable. Pre-existing content, not modified by TASK-011.

claims.claim: To disable: select the additional actions menu (...), then Select 'Disable'
claims.evidence: MemberList.cs: MaxVisibleRowActions=1, so only the edit action is directly visible. DisableMember command is in the overflow '...' menu. EnableDisableActionStateEvaluator displays 'Disable' label (base.members.actions.disable) when member is currently enabled.

claims.claim: To delete: select the 'Delete' action, confirm the deletion
claims.evidence: MemberList.cs: AddDeleteAction with confirmation (EnsureDeleteActionStatus sets confirmation title).

claims.claim: Member roles let you organize members into groups based on content access
claims.evidence: MemberRoleMemberInfo binding table (IsBinding=true). ContentAccessSettings.SecuredWithRoles() enables role-based content restrictions. ContentItemMemberRoleInfo links content items to roles.
claims.docFact: AREA-002: MemberRoleInfo and MemberRoleMemberInfo types support role creation and member-role assignment.

claims.claim: Member roles work together with the security settings available on pages and content items
claims.evidence: AREA-009: ContentItemFieldsSourceExtensions.HasAccess() extension, SecuredContentAccessValidator, MemberAccessHelper all connect member roles to content security. AREA-006: ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames property on query results.

claims.claim: Your development team must also implement the access checks on the front end for the restrictions to take effect
claims.evidence: HasAccess() is an extension method developers must call in their page/content rendering code. SecuredContentAccessValidator exists but must be wired into the MVC pipeline by the development team.

claims.claim: View roles: Open Members application → go to 'List of membership roles' tab
claims.evidence: MemberRoleList.cs: UIPage(typeof(MembersApplication), 'roles', typeof(MemberRoleList), '{$base.memberroles.list.caption$}', ...). Resource: base.memberroles.list.caption = 'List of membership roles'.
claims.docFact: AREA-012: Member role admin UI pages (listing/create/edit) already present.

claims.claim: The listing displays all member roles. Each role shows its membership role name.
claims.evidence: MemberRoleList.cs: ObjectType = MemberRoleInfo.OBJECT_TYPE. Single column: .AddColumn(nameof(MemberRoleInfo.MemberRoleDisplayName), ...) with searchable:true and label from base.memberroles.displayname = 'Membership role name'.

claims.claim: Select 'New membership role' to create a role
claims.evidence: MemberRoleList.cs line 34: PageConfiguration.HeaderActions.AddLink<MemberRoleCreate>(...'base.memberroles.newrole'). Resource: base.memberroles.newrole = 'New membership role'.

claims.claim: Enter a 'Membership role name' for the role
claims.evidence: create.xml line 12-20: MemberRoleDisplayName field, visible=true, order=2. Caption: '{$base.memberroles.displayName$}' = 'Membership role name'. Control: TextInput.

claims.claim: Optionally, expand the 'Code name' section to customize the automatically generated identifier
claims.location: Create a member role, step 5 (line 90) — THE FIX
claims.evidence: create.xml lines 22-32: MemberRoleName field, visible=true, order=3. Control: Kentico.Administration.CodeName. Settings: HasAutomaticCodeNameGenerationOption=True (auto-generates from display name), IsCollapsed=True (starts collapsed). Caption: '{$base.memberroles.codeName$}' = 'Code name'. The step correctly reflects: (a) optional action (auto-generates by default), (b) 'expand' needed (starts collapsed), (c) 'customize the automatically generated identifier' (has auto-generation).
claims.docFact: AREA-002: MemberRoleInfo CodeName field has HasAutomaticCodeNameGenerationOption=True, IsCollapsed=True

claims.claim: Optionally, add a 'Description' that explains the purpose of the role
claims.evidence: create.xml lines 34-46: MemberRoleDescription field, visible=true. Control: TextArea. Caption: '{$base.memberroles.description$}' = 'Description'. Field is optional (cms.memberrole.xml line 21: allowempty='true').

claims.claim: Edit role: update 'Membership role name', 'Code name', or 'Description' as needed
claims.evidence: edit.xml: All three fields visible=true. MemberRoleDisplayName (Membership role name), MemberRoleName (Code name, HasAutomaticCodeNameGenerationOption=False in edit mode, IsCollapsed=True), MemberRoleDescription (Description). All editable on the edit form. Code name starts collapsed but is directly editable — no auto-generation on edit.

claims.claim: Edit role: select the role from the listing to open it
claims.evidence: MemberRoleList.cs line 50: PageConfiguration.AddEditRowAction<MemberRoleEditSection>(...). MemberRoleEditSection.cs uses PARAMETERIZED_SLUG → routes to MemberRoleEdit page.

claims.claim: Assign members: in the 'Membership roles' section, select the roles to assign
claims.evidence: Member edit.xml: category 'Membership_roles' with caption '{$base.members.edit.roles.category$}' = 'Membership roles'. Field 'MemberRole' uses ObjectIdSelector with ObjectType='cms.memberrole'. MemberEdit.cs line 83: roleComponent = formItems.GetComponentByName<ObjectIdSelectorComponent>(ROLE_FIELD_NAME).

claims.claim: When a page or content item is restricted to one of these roles, only assigned members can view the content on the live site
claims.location: Assign members to roles, result paragraph (line 121)
claims.evidence: AREA-009: MemberAccessHelper.CheckAccess checks authentication + role membership → ContentAccessCheckResult (Allowed/NotAuthenticated/Forbidden). AREA-002: ContentAccessSettings.SecuredWithRoles() model.

claims.claim: To remove a member from a role, open the member's detail page, remove the role from the 'Membership roles' section, and select Save
claims.location: Assign members to roles, removal instructions (line 123)
claims.evidence: MemberEdit.cs lines 97-118: UpdateMemberRoles() computes rolesToRemove = existingRoles.Except(roles), then BulkDeletes the removed bindings within a transaction scope.

claims.claim: Page link targets: 8oouCw (Secure pages), content_items_xp (content items), member_roles_xp (Member roles dev docs), tYouCw (Registration and authentication)
claims.evidence: All page_link identifiers resolve: 8oouCw → business-users/website-content/secure-pages.md; content_items_xp → business-users/content-hub/content-items.md; member_roles_xp → developers-and-admins/development/registration-and-authentication/member-roles.md; tYouCw → developers-and-admins/development/registration-and-authentication.md.

claims.claim: related_pages frontmatter includes 8oouCw, content_items_xp, member_roles_xp
claims.evidence: Line 10: related_pages: ['8oouCw', 'content_items_xp', 'member_roles_xp']. All three identifiers exist in the docs workspace.

researchRecommendationChecks.recommendation: Location-before-action pattern, 8-step max, Save as explicit step
researchRecommendationChecks.notes: All procedures follow location→action pattern (e.g., 'Open the Members application' → 'Go to the List of membership roles tab'). Create procedure has 7 steps. Edit has 5. Assign has 4. Save is always an explicit step.

researchRecommendationChecks.recommendation: Arrow notation for navigation; 'Open the [App] application' pattern
researchRecommendationChecks.notes: Uses 'Open the **Members** application' pattern consistently. Navigation is via numbered steps rather than inline arrow notation, which is acceptable — no multi-level nav paths requiring arrow syntax appear.

researchRecommendationChecks.recommendation: Consistent Kentico terminology: member, visitor, sign in, register
researchRecommendationChecks.notes: Doc uses 'member' for registered visitors, 'visitor' for anonymous users (line 50), 'sign in' consistently (lines 29-31, 48, 50). No instances of 'log in', 'login', 'sign up', or 'signup' found.

fixVerification.previousRejection: Step 5 in 'Create a member role' said 'Enter a Code name' implying manual entry is required
fixVerification.fix: Step 5 changed to 'Optionally, expand the Code name section to customize the automatically generated identifier'
fixVerification.verdict: Fix is correct. The create form's Code name field auto-generates (HasAutomaticCodeNameGenerationOption=True) and starts collapsed (IsCollapsed=True). The new step correctly reflects all three aspects: optional (auto-generates), requires expanding (collapsed), customizes an auto-generated value.

regressionCheck.editFormCodeName: edit.xml has HasAutomaticCodeNameGenerationOption=False, IsCollapsed=True. Doc line 104 lists Code name as editable field — correct for edit scenario where auto-generation is off.
regressionCheck.notes: All 25 claims verified against source code. No pre-existing inaccuracies introduced or amplified by TASK-011 changes.
